source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About us

At Jigsaw, we’re on a mission to transform the way professional services and financial institutions work. We do this by helping our clients organise, visualise and use their data (think Figma or Canva but for the ‘City’). Our clients include the world’s biggest consultancies, leading financial institutions and top law firms.  

Jigsaw was founded by two seasoned entrepreneurs, Stephen and Travis. This is their second venture together; their first was acquired by a US private equity firm and went on to become a billion-dollar company and a household name in professional service firms across the world. Jigsaw is now on a growth trajectory few Series A companies achieve - putting us in the top quartile SaaS companies globally. Today we proudly serve thousands of users from nearly two  hundred businesses worldwide, who love our products and use them everyday. 

But we’re only getting started! We have ambitious goals and we’re looking for talented people to help us achieve them. 

  

Why join us? 

We're not about the corporate grind; instead, we thrive on creativity, enthusiasm, and a touch of humour to make magic happen. 

We hire people with high ownership and a bias for action. Then we trust and empower them to do their best work. 




THE ROLE

The primary role will be to seek new business opportunities by contacting and developing relationships with potential customers. You will use your initiative to discover leads, reaching out to prospects and building interest in Jigsaw. You will work closely with the Business Development team, executing the Account Executives’ targeted campaigns, as well as independently driving your own outreach.

Working in a startup presents variety and multiple opportunities to take on additional responsibilities to expand beyond your normal role. Things can move fast and we’re

looking for someone flexible and driven, with a natural ability to learn and quickly adapt. This is a fantastic opportunity to springboard into the exciting world of entrepreneurialism, offering potential for fast career growth and in-depth exposure to starting and growing a business.

This is not just a job. If you are motivated, enjoy working in a dynamic team environment and would like to be part of the technology revolution, we’d like you to join us on our journey.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Proactively seek new business opportunities in the market

 - Contact potential clients through cold calls and emails

 - Set up meetings or calls between (prospective) clients and Account Executives

 - Report on (weekly/monthly/quarterly) sales results
   
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - Excellent communication and people skills, including the ability to ‘think on your feet’

 - Ability to organize and prioritize workload

 - Proven track record of achieving and exceeding goals

 - Adaptability and resilience

 - Prior Business Development experience is a bonus

 - Based in or willing to relocate to London area

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About us

At Jigsaw, we’re on a mission to transform the way professional services and financial institutions work. We do this by helping our clients organise, visualise and use their data (think Figma or Canva but for the ‘City’). Our clients include the world’s biggest consultancies, leading financial institutions and top law firms.  

Jigsaw was founded by two seasoned entrepreneurs, Stephen and Travis. This is their second venture together; their first was acquired by a US private equity firm and went on to become a billion-dollar company and a household name in professional service firms across the world. Jigsaw is now on a growth trajectory few Series A companies achieve - putting us in the top quartile SaaS companies globally. Today we proudly serve thousands of users from nearly two  hundred businesses worldwide, who love our products and use them everyday. 

But we’re only getting started! We have ambitious goals and we’re looking for talented people to help us achieve them. 

  

Why join us? 

We're not about the corporate grind; instead, we thrive on creativity, enthusiasm, and a touch of humour to make magic happen. 

We hire people with high ownership and a bias for action. Then we trust and empower them to do their best work. 


About the role
We’re seeking a driven and commercially minded Account Executive to join our growing US team. Trusted by 80% of AmLaw 100 firms, Jigsaw helps legal professionals create diagrams 60x faster than legacy tools such as PowerPoint or Visio. We've made serious inroads in the market, and now we need someone exceptional to grow our customer base.

You'll be based in our New York office, working across the mid-market US legal sector, playing a significant part in the continued growth of Jigsaw. This role comes with real responsibility, real earning potential, and a team that is fully invested in helping you succeed.

You'll get to...

 - Own your own book of business, taking prospective customers through the full sales cycle from prospecting to close

 - Sell a product that partners genuinely love, into a market that is still largely running on legacy tools

 - Build real relationships with senior stakeholders at law firms across the US, understand their world and work closely with partners who are acting on some of the largest transactions and legal cases in the world.

 - Become an excellent natural salesperson - we are skill-based, not methodology-dependent, and the way we sell here will help you grow your capabilities

 - Travel regularly across the US to meet clients in person, with trips to our wider global team in Europe a couple of times a year

 - Be part of an ambitious team where progression is fast for people who deliver, with leadership accessible when you need support

You'll thrive in this role if...

 - You have 1-2 years experience closing deals. A SaaS background is advantageous but not required; we are just as interested in someone from an adjacent industry where you’ve supported customers through a buying process

 - You are motivated by building your own pipeline and managing your own book of business

 - You are coachable and genuinely open to learning a new way of doing things - we have a specific, proven sales process and will teach you everything you need to know in order to execute it

 - You can manage high volume without losing the detail - you’ll be working with a substantial number of accounts simultaneously, each needing consistent, thoughtful follow-up

 - You are independent enough to operate without hand-holding, but self-aware enough to ask for help when you need it

 - You are willing to travel regularly within the US. In-person visits to clients are an important part of this role

 - Familiarity with the US legal sector, including how law firms are structured and make decisions or experience selling into a similarly specialised professional services environment would be a strong plus, but not a dealbreaker

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About us

At Jigsaw, we’re on a mission to transform the way professional services and financial institutions work. We do this by helping our clients organise, visualise and use their data (think Figma or Canva but for the ‘City’). Our clients include the world’s biggest consultancies, leading financial institutions and top law firms.  

Jigsaw was founded by two seasoned entrepreneurs, Stephen and Travis. This is their second venture together; their first was acquired by a US private equity firm and went on to become a billion-dollar company and a household name in professional service firms across the world. Jigsaw is now on a growth trajectory few Series A companies achieve - putting us in the top quartile SaaS companies globally. Today we proudly serve thousands of users from nearly two  hundred businesses worldwide, who love our products and use them everyday. 

But we’re only getting started! We have ambitious goals and we’re looking for talented people to help us achieve them. 

  

Why join us? 

We're not about the corporate grind; instead, we thrive on creativity, enthusiasm, and a touch of humour to make magic happen. 

We hire people with high ownership and a bias for action. Then we trust and empower them to do their best work. 


The role

Jigsaw is seeking a talented and experienced Senior Product Designer to join our growing team. You will play a critical role in shaping the user interface and user experience of our design tool, ensuring that legal, accounting and other professionals can efficiently visualise complex legal structures, processes, and relationships.



YOU’LL GET TO…

 - Design interfaces that help users explore complex, interconnected data (entity hierarchies, ownership chains, multi-level relationships, etc.) where users need both the detail and the big picture at the same time

 - Build high-density views (tables, trees, relationship charts, timelines) that are scannable, structured, and not overwhelming

 - Engage with how data actually works (how records relate to each other, what happens when data is missing or contradictory) so designs hold up against real-world complexity

 - Run discovery with users - lawyers, accountants, compliance professionals - to understand where the current product loses them and what they actually need from a visualisation tool

 - Work closely with product and engineering to make design decisions that hold up technically, not just in Figma

 - Contribute to and evolving Jigsaw's design system, so the patterns you create become reusable building blocks


YOU'LL THRIVE IN THIS ROLE IF...

 - You have 5+ years of product design experience in SaaS, and a portfolio that shows your process, not just your output

 - You can make dense information feel manageable - knowing when to show, when to hide, and how to guide someone's eye

 - You have strong problem-solving skills and the ability to simplify complex concepts through design

 - You are proficient in design and prototyping tools such as Figma, Sketch, Adobe XD, or similar

 - You have a good understanding of accessibility and UX best practices

 - You know the legal industry or you have experience designing for a specialised domain (this would be a plus; not required)

notes: Highly sales-oriented job postings with a single product designer role, indicating a strong focus on business development and growth.
